sutta: the buddha said monks do you live warded not waterless sorrowful sorrowfully dwells the waterless monk stop here for a moment this word warded means protected so what the buddha is saying is monks do you live warded or protected not unprotected sorrowfully dwells the unprotected monk so the buddha is trying to tell the monks that you must do certain things to get protection then the buddha continued monks there are these 10 states that make forwarding or protection what then hearing among is virtuous restrained with the restraint of the patimoka perfect in conduct and resort seeing danger in the slightest false and undertaking the precepts trains himself therein in so far as a monk is such a 1 this is a state that makes for then again among has heard much he bears in mind what he has heard he stores up what he has heard whatsoever teachings lovely at the beginning lovely midway lovely at the end in spirit and letter to stress the holy life in all its fullness and utter purity such teachings are much heard by him born in mind repeated aloud pondered and well penetrated by vision of you insofar as a monk is such a 1 this tool is a state that makes for warding then again among has a good friend lyanna mitta a good ally a good companion and so far as a monk has such friendship this tool is a state that makes forwarding again among is pleasant to speak to he is blessed with qualities that make him easy to speak to he is patient and clever at grasping instruction given insofar as among is such this tool is a state that makes for warding then again in all the undertakings of his companions in the holy life the day matters weighty or trivial he is shrewd and energetic possessing ability to give proper consideration there too as to what is the right thing to do and how to manage it and so far as among is such a 1 this tool is a state that makes forwarding then again among delights in dhamma is pleasant to converse with rejoices exceedingly in higher dharma and higher vinaya insofar as a man is such a 1 this state also makes for warning again among dwells resolute in energy for the abandonment of bad qualities stout and strong to acquire good qualities not shirking the burden in good qualities insofar as a monk is such a 1 this too is a state that makes for warding again among is content with whatsoever supply of robes arms food lodging comforts and medicines for sickness he may get insofar as the monk is such a 1 this tool is a state that makes for warding again a mom has recollection possessed of supreme recollection and prudence able to call to mind and remember things done and said long ago this tool is a state that makes for warding again monks among this possessed of wisdom he has wisdom for seeing the rise and fall of things wisdom which is aryan penetrating going to the utter destruction of ill insofar as among has all these 10 qualities this tool is a state that makes for warding monks do you live warded not borderless sorrowful dwells the wardless monks these then are the 10 states that make for warding now that's the end of the suta
commentary:
now i'll commend the first 1 that makes for protection is virtue or moral conduct and the monk is here is mentioned the patimoka patimoca is the code of precepts and 227 that among is supposed to hold as best as he can because it's not possible for a monk to be perfect in all the precepts and so however well he can uphold the precepts he is supposed to try as best as he can then so if a monk has virtua this is a statement that makes for protection the second 1 the monk has much learning usually there is a word that occurs in the sutras quite often it's called bahu sacha and sometimes means much hearing much hearing means much hearing of the dhamma or much hearing of the suttas the discourses of the buddha and then bahu sacha bahu means much sacha means truths so it also means much hearing of the truth so here the second quality here for a manga a good second good quality among should have is to hear much of the sutas much of the dhamma and remember or [Music] repeat so that memorizer so that he can remember then the this uh much learning is very important especially nowadays because there's a lot of wrong teachings so if a person wants to practice the holy life he should now be very familiar with the original teachings of the buddha so that he can have right view and not get wrong viewer from some other teacher and the third quality is to have a good friend good friend is a kalyana mitta now in chinese they translate that as a good knowing advisor but actually literally it means a good friend that means a good monk friend and normally it refers to a monk teacher among teacher who can guide you along the right path of practice so in 1 of the sutas the rebel ananda said half of the holy life has to do with having a good friend a good man teacher and the buddha said not so and the buddha said all of the holy life has to do with having a good monk teacher so having a good teacher is very important because firstly he will guide you to the right teachings and the the right teachings of the buddha secondly from his experience he can save you a lot of time because he himself has gone through many years of practice and he has wasted much time from not having a good teacher perhaps and then if he if you obtain guidance from somebody with experience then you'll save a lot of time and effort that's number 3 number 4 is uh among is pleasant to speak to he has he's blessed with qualities that make him easy to speak to he's patient and clever at grasping instruction given so this 1 is to be easy to talk to like easy to give instructions to like there are some people who are very stubborn who don't take instructions that is an obstruction if you are the type of the person then the teacher is not inclined to give you much instruction because you don't listen you don't obey so having this quality of being easy to talk to easy to teach is a state that makes for protection then number 5 he is shrewd and energetic possessing ability to give proper consideration to the undertakings of his companions in the holy life sometimes when monks stay together they have duties to do so among who is able to help her by carrying out his duties properly and then that is a good quality to have and then his companion shipment will be much valued by the other monks and number 6 uh among the lights in dhamma delights in the higher dharma and higher vinaya the higher dharma here is the the empali is called and usually when the word abhidhamma occurs in the sutas it comes together with abhi vinaya the higher dhamma and the higher vinaya and this higher dhamma does not refer to the 7 books of the abhidharma that nowadays some monks teach those 7 books of the abhidharma came later this higher dharma refers to the dhamma that you practice and to get out of samsara and sometimes the buddha does mention that it refers to the 37 bodhi then the 5 bala the 5 indian the 7 bojanga and the noble eightfold path uh this is the uh things that you cultivate and to get out of samsara so this is supposed to be the higher dharma and the higher vidalia is the higher discipline which is more for monks number 7 the manga dwells resolute in energy for the abandonment of bad qualities and to acquire good qualities so this 1 uh making a strong effort to progress this is another good quality that makes for protection number 8 to be content with whatever requisites that he obtains to be contented number 9 is to have sati here you can see the standard definition of sati he has recollection possessed of supreme recollection and prudence able to call to mind and remember things done and said long ago very often nowadays they translate sati as mindfulness but from the definition of sati here you can see it means being able to remember what was said and done a long time ago so it is the quality of remembering so recollection is a good word because it means the same thing as remembering now what do you mean by recollection recollection means calling to mind calling to mind remembering that means remembering to practice and in the practice of sati basically the buddha advice and that we should put our mind on a useful object of contemplation if we don't put our mind on the useful object of contemplation the mind the mind tends to run all over the place be very scattered and think of worldly things so that is why satya is calling to mind 4 things like contemplate 4 things the body feelings the nature of the mind and dharma the buddha's teachings so that is the uh purpose of satiella is to put our mind to remember to put our mind on the right place in the right place and not let it be scattered and then number 10 now is to have wisdom and usually wisdom as is shown here refers to having the wisdom to see the arising and the seizing of everything in this world because if you can see the rising and the seizing of everything either physical or mental then you will see that there is no security in this world everything is falling apart everything is born and ceases born and dies away uh so because that is the nature of the world impermanence and then uh you begin to realize it it is a frightening world there is no security no permanence in anything so that is the wisdom the buddhas the buddha's notion of wisdom is not like worldly wisdom worldly wisdom is to know a lot of things but no other buddhas wisdom is concerned with seeing impermanence and because of impermanence which is suffering or stress or distress stop here for a moment so that's now those 10 qualities if among possesses it helps to protect him i'll just repeat that the first 1 is having virtue or moral conduct the second 1 is to have much learning how much knowledge of the buddha's teachings the original buddha's teachings the third 1 is to have a good friend or a good teacher and the fourth 1 is being pleasant to speak to pleasant to instruct then the number 5 is to have the ability and to carry out uh duties of the sangha and then number 6 is to have to delight in the dhamma to have an affinity for the dhamma number 7 is to be very energetic to practice or cultivate them and abandon bad qualities and acquire good qualities number 8 is to be content number 9 is to have sati recollection number 10 is to have wisdom

sutta: once the exalted 1 was staying among the gurus at kama sadama the township of the kurus on that occasion the exalted 1 addressed the monks saying monks there are these 10 ways of aryan living or abiding according to which aryans have lived do live and shall live what can hearing among has abandoned 5 factors is possessed of 6 factors god's 1 factor observes the 4 bases or supports has got rid of individual beliefs or views has utterly given up longings his thoughts are unclouded his body conditioner is tranquilized he is well released by mind he is well released by wisdom these are the 10 ways of aryan living according to which aryans have lived do live and shall live i'll just stop here for a moment so here what the buddha is saying is that aryans practice these 10 things but these but it doesn't mean that all aryans possess these 10 things uh the highest aryan which is the arahant would possess all these 10 qualities but other aryans for example the sotapanna or sakura the garmin they would have slightly they may have slightly less than all these 10 qualities just like the noble eightfold path if a manga possess the noble eightfold path completely then he is an arahan but other aryans do not possess the noble eiffel path completely or perfectly and then the buddha continued [Music] and how is among 1 who has abandoned 5 factors hearing among has abandoned sensual desire ill will sloth and topper restlessness and worry doubt and wavering thus he is 1 who has abandoned 5 factors and how is among 1 who is possessed of 6 factors herein among seeing an object with the eye is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected hearing a sound with the ear smelling ascent with a nose tasting a flavor with a tongue contacting an object with the body with mind cognizing mental states he is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected thus is among possessed of 6 factors and how does a monk guard 1 factor by guarding recollection or sometimes they translate as mindfulness he is composed or collected thus he guards 1 factor and how does a monk observe the 4 bases herein among deliberately develops 1 thing deliberately nurtures or promotes another thing restrains 1 thing [Music] abandons or suppresses another thing thus among observes the 4 bases and how is among 1 who has got rid of individual beliefs or views iran monks whatsoever individual views generally prevail among recluses and brahmins namely the world is eternal the world is not eternal the world is finite it is infinite the soul and the body are the same the soul and the body are different exists after death but the targatha exists not after that or he both exists and yet exists not or he neither exists nor exists not after death all these views are shaken off put away given up let go abandoned and dismissed thus is among 1 who has got rid of individual views and how is among 1 who has utterly given up longings hearing in among longing for things sensual is abandoned longing for existence is abandoned longing for practicing the holy life has calmed down thus is among 1 who has utterly given up longings and how are amongst thoughts unclouded hearing among has abandoned thoughts sensual thoughts malicious thoughts of harming thus are his thoughts unclouded and how is a monk's body conditioner tranquilized hearing among by abandoning pleasure and pain by coming to an end of the ease and discomfort that which he had before attains and abides in the state of neither pain nor pleasure and equan and utter economy utter economy equanimity which is the fourth jhana thus his body conditioner is tranquilized and housed among well released by mind hearing amongst mind is released from passion hate and delusion or great agreed hatred and delusion and how is among well released by wisdom herein among knows for certain greed or passion is abandoned in me cut off at the root made like a palm tree stump made not to become again of a nature not to arise again in future hatred delusion is abandoned in me not to arise again in future time thus is among well released by wisdom monks whatsoever aryans have in the past lived according to the aryan living all of them lived according to these 10 ways of aryan living whatsoever aryans shall in future time so live whatsoever aarons do now so live all of them shall live and do live according to these 10 ways of aryan living these monks are the 10 ways of aryan living and that's the end of the suta
commentary: now it is explained the first 1 among has abandoned 5 factors that means sensual desire ill will sloth and topper restlessness and doubt now these 5 things are called the 5 hindrances and these are the things that obstruct wisdom the buddha said these are evil things only when you get rid of these 5 hindrances can you have wisdom because they obstruct you from seeing things clearly from seeing things as they really are now how do you get rid of these 5 hindrances in a suta in the majima nikaya the buddha did explain that you must attain the jhana to get rid of the 5 hindrances so the first 1 is to abandon the 5 hindrances the second 1 among is possessed of 6 factors seeing an object with the eye is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected and similarly with hearing the sound smelling as smell etc so here it means guarding uh mindful of the sixth sense doors and practicing here in bali is called sato sampajano sato refers to sati having recollection and sam pajano means mindfulness or full awareness full awareness these 2 terms tend to come together because when you practice mindfulness and then you tend to have satie means having full awareness or mindfulness and sati is to remember to put your mind in the right direction at the 4 objects of satir then number 3 is to guard 1 factor here this factor is also sati sati recollection this is guarding 1 factor sati is quite important for a man who is practicing putting the mind on the body the feelings the mind and the dhamma and number 4 how uh is to observe the 4 bases these 4 bases are the 4 right efforts uh deliberately develop 1 thing i deliberately nurture or promote another thing restrain or avoid 1 thing abandon or suppress another thing this refers to wholesome states and unwholesome states the first 1 is uh wholesome states who some states that have not developed you must develop it and they have not arisen you have to develop it so that it arises and then wholesome states that have already arisen you should mature or promote it so that it continues to arise then unwholesome states that have not arisen you should avoid or restrain it from arising and unwholesome states which have already arisen you try to suppress it or abandon it so these are the full right efforts and number 5 the monk has got rid of individual views all kinds of views exist in the world that the world is eternal the world is not eternal the world is finite or infinite etc these all have to do with an ego once you have a view of a self then you tend to think about all these things like so the monk has got rid of all these views number 6 is to have given up longings here longings refers to sensual longings longing for existence and longing for practicing the holy life has calmed down the first 1 you can understand sensual of uh objects and longing for sensual things you're abandoned longing for existence also you abandon them when you become an arya but the third 1 longing for practicing the holy life has come down and this 1 looks a bit strange in the first suta of the sanguita nikaya the buddha said that some deva asked him how did you cross the flood and the buddha said not striving and not standing still i crossed the flood the buddha said when i strove i was swirled about when i stood still i sang thus without striving without standing still i crossed the flood what the buddha was meant was if he made too much effort then he was swirled around if you make too much effort but if you make no effort at all also that means he sang in the middle of the suppose a person is trying to cross the river you know he make too much effort he'll go in a circle so if he made no effort he'll be he'll sing in the water so the buddha said neither striving nor standing still he crossed the flood in other words to have an equanimous mind if you have too much desire to get liberated for a monk that is also bad having no desire to get liberated all so bad so uh the mind must be equanimous and then oniya he could uh cross the flood number 7 how are amongst thoughts unclouded a man among has abandoned 4 central thoughts malicious and thoughts of harming these are the wrong thoughts so he has abandoned wrong thoughts and so his thoughts are unclouded and number 9 his body conditioner is tranquilized this 1 um refers the body conditioner refers to the breath because the breath is conditioned by the sorry the body is conditioned by the breath and the body exists because of the because of the breath so the body conditioner remain refers to the breath and here it means the the breath has stopped because when the person attains the fourth jana the breath stops so here uh this aryan has attained the fourth jana and normally uh that is a condition for an anagaming and an arahant uh he must an anagami a third fruition and an arahant fourth fruition arya they must attain the 4 jaanas and then number 9 a monk is released by mind here he is released from greed hatred and delusion or passion hatred and delusion and then how is among released by wisdom also he is released from passion or greed hatred and delusion so these are the 10 things that aryans practice and and all they attain to become aryans some of them you find is uh refers only to the arahant for the for example the last 2 released or liberated by mind and released or liberated by wisdom this only refers to the arahant now the next suta is number 27

sutta: once the exalted one was staying near savati at jeter grove in anathapindika park. on that occasion, the number of monks, after returning from their arms round and eating their meal, had gathered and sat down in the assembly hall. there they stayed indulging in diverse sorts of aimless talk about such things as rajas or kings, robbers and ministers, talk of armies, dangers and battles, talk of food and drink, clothes, beds, garlands and perfumes, talk of relatives, vehicles, villages, townships, cities and countries, talk about women and heroes, about streets and gossip at the well, go stories, trifles and about the origin of land and sea and whether things exist or do not exist. stop here for a moment. here the monks were talking about this all this aimless talk which is called tiracana kata. it means animal talk. why is it called animal talk? because it is like base, low. animal is considered very low. so this low type of talk. then the exalted one rising from his solitude in the evening went towards the assembly hall and on reaching it sat down on a seat made ready. being seated, the exalted one addressed the monks saying, monks, on what subjects were you conversing gathered together here and what was the nature of the talk left unfinished by you. and they said, sir, after returning from our arms round and eating our meal, we gathered and sat down in the assembly hall and so stayed indulging in diverse sorts of pointless talks such as rajas, etc. and the buddha said, monks, it is not seemly that you clansmen who in faith have gone forth from the home to the homeless life should indulge in such talk. there are these 10 topics of talk. what 10? talk about having few ones, about contentment, seclusion, solitude, energetic striving, virtue, concentration, wisdom, release or liberation, and knowing and seeing release. these monks are the 10 topics of talk. monks, if you should engage again and again in talk on these 10 topics, you would outshine in brilliance even the brilliance of the moon and sun, which are of such mighty power and majesty, not to speak of the brilliance of the wanderers who hold other views. that's the end of the sutta.
commentary: so here the buddha is encouraging the monks to talk on 10 topics which are very beneficial to them. the first one is having few ones, or wanting little or having few ones, not wanting many things, about contentment, about seclusion. seclusion is going to a quiet place to live, for example, in a forest monastery or in a cave. and then solitude. solitude is like being, keeping to yourself. for example, in a monastery, there might be many monks. so if a monk wants to practice very hard, he doesn't want to talk a lot with other monks. he keeps to himself. and then energetic striving, using a lot of energy to practice. and then virtue. virtue, this moral conduct, la sila, concentration, wisdom, and then liberation, and knowing and seeing liberation. so these are the 10 types of talks which are very conducive to progress in the holy life. now the next sutta

to 1 who delights in seclusion delight in society is a thorn to 1 devoted to concentration on the sign of the unattractiveness concentration on the sign of the attractiveness is a thorn to 1 guarding the doors of the sense faculties the sight of shows is a thorn to the holy life consorting with women is a thorn to the first jhana noise is a torn to the second jhana thought directed and sustained is a torn to the third jhana delight or pity is a torn to the fourth jhana breathing in breathing and out breathing is a thorn through the attainment of cessation of perception and feeling perception and feeling are a thorn lust hatred and delusion are thorns so monks do do you abide thornless do you abide thorn removers do you abide thoughtless thorn removers monks the thornless are arahants the thornless thorn removers are arahants and that's the end of the gupta

so here the buddha is saying and for a monk who delights in seclusion delight in society is a torn to 1 devoted to concentration on the sign of the unattractiveness here it means the unattractiveness of the body and then the sign of the attractiveness of the body is a torn to progress in that meditation to 1 guarding the doors of the sense faculties the sight of shows seeing shows like tv and all that is a torn to the holy life consorting with women is a torn and this 1 is is quite a fact because many monks who disrobe they do so because of some women many times among these ropes because of having become too familiar with some women and then some emotion or feeling develops and then sometimes the monk he finds the holy life quite difficult so sometimes they disrobe and marry some woman to the first jhana thoughts noise is a torn if if you want to attain the first jhana you must stay in a quiet place you stay in a city area with a lot of sounds and all that is very difficult to attain to the second jhana thought directed and sustained because thought directed and sustained is is 1 of the factors of the first jhana so when you want to attain to the second jana you must let go of that those thoughts to the third jana the light pity is a torn because in the third jana there's no more delight in the for the fourth jana breathing is a torn because a person who attains a fort jana the breathing stops her and then to the attainment of the cessation of perception and feeling uh perception and feeling are a thorn this cessation of perception and feeling is uh just like nibana where the consciousness stops so uh when the normal sex consciousness when the normal sex consciousness stops her and then this perception and feeling also stopped so if you still have perception and feeling that means you cannot enter that that state of cessation and then lust hatred and delusion so if we till the buddha said if a man gets rid of all these thorns then he can become an arahant

sutta: the buddha said monks by increasing in in by increasing in 10 growths the aryan disciple grows in the aryan growth takes whole of the essential takes hold of the best for his person what then he grows in landed property in wealth and greenery in child and wife in slaves and folk who work for him in 4-footed beasts he grows in faith and virtue in love generosity and wisdom in these 10 growths the aryan disciple grows in the aryan growth takes hold of the essential takes hold of the best for his person that's the end of the suta

items: slaves and folk who work for him

sutta: the buddha said monks 3 ideas of a recluse if made to grow made much of complete 7 other conditions what 3 1 i am now come to a state of being an outcast 2 my life is dependent upon others number 3 i must now conduct myself differently these 3 ideas of a recluse if made to grow made much of complete 7 other conditions what 7 1 he is 1 whoever and always makes no pause in action or practice of virtue he does no harm to anyone he is without self-conceit he is eager for the training as regards the necessaries of life he thinks this is my object he dwells arden in energy thus monks these 3 ideas of a recluse if made to grow you've made much of complete these 7 conditions so now that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so here the buddha is saying that a manga should contemplate these 3 important things first that he has come he has now come to the state of being an outcast that means an outcast from society a person who is not somebody in society so he must always remember that because if he remembers that nah then he he will act quite differently number 2 he must remember my life is dependent upon others 3 i must now conduct myself differently now if even understands these 3 things and then he humbles himself he knows that his life is in the hands of lay supporters and then he will behave quite differently so these 7 things the buddha says he will practice number 1 he makes no pause in action here action is satata kari he makes no pause in action i don't know whether that means he's very energetic in striving number 2 he makes he he makes no pause in the practice of virtue in his precepts he he remembers to uphold his precepts number 3 he does no harm to anyone number 4 he is without self-conceit he becomes a humble person number 5 is eager for the training the training is the buddha's training in the noble eightfold path of sila samadhi pannier and number 6 as regards the necessaries of life he thinks this is my object in other words he thinks my object is practicing the holy life as far as the requisites of life and they are not so important whether i get a good rope or i eat good food or i sleep in a very comfortable bed or something not so important number 7 he dwells arden in energy that means he's uh very energetic in cultivating the holy life so these 3 conditions is very good for a monk to remember
